data.title: Document server architecture and run steps in root README

data.body: README rewritten per plan (92 lines). Verified port/env vars, copy-static.sh behavior, and HTTP surface against source before writing. All file references use markdown links. Flagged the font-size=320 and per-input viewBox deviations in the architecture section as instructed. Staged only README.md; Lattice planning artifacts left staged separately for the orchestrator.

acceptance criteria met

Verified against plan and source:
- Build verification: copy-static.sh + go build ./cmd/server succeeded.
- HTTP surface in README matches router.go exactly: GET /healthz, POST /api/render, GET|HEAD /api/files/{hash}.png, static fall-through.
- Env vars (PORT, DATA_DIR with default ./data/files) match cmd/server/main.go.
- copy-static.sh description matches the actual script (target dir server/internal/httpserver/static).
- All required architecture bullets present, both implementation deviations (font-size=320, per-input viewBox) flagged with rationale as required.
- All internal file/path references wrapped in markdown link syntax. Allowed bare/external links (thankyoubag.online, resvg-go GitHub, pkg.go.dev/singleflight) are appropriate.
- No fictional features (Printful correctly deferred, no Docker/deploy promised). node_modules/ not mentioned. No code snippets dumped.
- Length: 92 lines, slightly under 100-150 target but content is dense and well-scoped — not worth padding.
- Reads well; architecture is opinionated rather than exhaustive (e.g., 'load-bearing decision', 'Glue server, not a render engine').

No fixes needed.
